metadata.title: create-worktree
metadata.description: Create a new branch and linked Git worktree for isolated or parallel work without changing the current checkout.

command.goal:
Create a new branch and linked Git worktree for isolated or parallel work without changing the current checkout.
## Usage
/create-worktree <issue-or-change|type description> [<target-path>] [<base-reference>]
## Accepted Inputs
- An issue/change identifier, or an explicit branch type and description - Optional target path - Optional base reference - Supported branch types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `refactor`, and `chore`
## Owning Agent
@robot-tech-lead
## Associated Capabilities
- Git branch naming and validation - Git linked worktrees - Parallel OpenSpec child-change coordination

step.step-content: Resolve branch name, target path, and base reference.
Verify repository state and availability of branch and path.
Create worktree using branch creation semantics.
Report created branch, absolute path, and base reference.

output-format-list.output-format-item: Created local branch and linked worktree
Exact branch name, absolute path, and base reference

safeguards-list.safeguards-item: Stop if the branch exists, is checked out, or target path exists.
Do not commit, push, remove worktrees, delete branches, or use force.
